item.title: Can power lines really light a bulb in your hand?
item.description: Discover why a light bulb can glow under high-voltage power lines. But does this strange effect mean power lines are actually dangerous?

media:content.media:title: Can power lines really light a bulb in your hand?
media:content.media:description: Discover why a light bulb can glow under high-voltage power lines. But does this strange effect mean power lines are actually dangerous?

item.title: What's a gömböc and why it shouldn't exist?!
item.description: A look at the gömböc, a rare shape in physics and math that seems not to exist, yet does.

media:content.media:title: What's a gömböc and why it shouldn't exist?!
media:content.media:description: A look at the gömböc, a rare shape in physics and math that seems not to exist, yet does.

item.title: How salt fingering helps the oceans
item.description: A simple look at salt fingering, a physics and chemistry process that helps shape ocean mixing.

media:content.media:title: How salt fingering helps the oceans
media:content.media:description: A simple look at salt fingering, a physics and chemistry process that helps shape ocean mixing.

item.title: Can you cook an egg just by dropping it?
item.description: A simple physics experiment asks whether an egg can cook from a drop alone

media:content.media:title: Can you cook an egg just by dropping it?
media:content.media:description: A simple physics experiment asks whether an egg can cook from a drop alone

item.title: road trip to see my brothers!
item.description: road trip to see my brothers!

media:content.media:title: road trip to see my brothers!
media:content.media:description: road trip to see my brothers!

item.title: Is using ice the best choice to cool something down?
item.description: A simple science question: is ice the best choice for cooling something down? A clear, educational experiment on heat transfer.

media:content.media:title: Is using ice the best choice to cool something down?
media:content.media:description: A simple science question: is ice the best choice for cooling something down? A clear, educational experiment on heat transfer.

item.title: 1 giant magnet vs 1,000 small magnets
item.description: In this video I see what happens when I let 1 giant monster neodymium magnet collide with 1,000 tiny neodymium magnets! Then I test what happens when I try to hold them in my hand next to the giant neodymium magnet!

media:content.media:title: 1 giant magnet vs 1,000 small magnets
media:content.media:description: In this video I see what happens when I let 1 giant monster neodymium magnet collide with 1,000 tiny neodymium magnets! Then I test what happens when I try to hold them in my hand next to the giant neodymium magnet!

item.title: Opening a vacuum chamber under water!
item.description: In this video I sho you what happens when you open a vacuum chamber under water. Also, due to many questions about this, I test if a vacuum chamber floats!

media:content.media:title: Opening a vacuum chamber under water!
media:content.media:description: In this video I sho you what happens when you open a vacuum chamber under water. Also, due to many questions about this, I test if a vacuum chamber floats!
